title: Salade de Lentilles

overview.description: A warm French lentil salad with green lentils, carrots, onion, fresh herbs and a sharp mustard vinaigrette.

method: Place the lentils in a saucepan with the crushed garlic, bay leaves, thyme and stock or water.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 20–30 minutes until the lentils are tender but still retain their shape.
While the lentils cook, heat the olive oil in a large frying pan.
Add the diced carrot and onion.
Cook gently for 8–10 minutes until softened but not browned.
Remove the bay leaves, thyme stems and garlic from the cooked lentils.
Whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, shallot, salt and black pepper.
Add the warm lentils to the cooked carrot and onion.
Pour over the dressing while the lentils are still warm.
Toss gently so the lentils absorb the dressing.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

learning.did-you-know: Lentil salads are particularly associated with French home cooking, and Puy lentils are prized because they retain their shape and have a distinctive peppery flavour.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook lentils so they retain their texture and how warm ingredients absorb vinaigrette more effectively than cold ones.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A warming, inexpensive complete meal salad that works particularly well in colder weather and makes excellent use of the lentils already in the pantry.
chapman-tips: Don't overcook the lentils; they should retain their shape.
Dress the lentils while they are still warm so they absorb the vinaigrette.
Puy lentils are ideal but ordinary green lentils work very well.
The mustard and vinegar should provide noticeable sharpness to balance the earthy lentils.
Don't add salt to the cooking water if using a salty stock; season the finished dish instead.
Serve with good crusty bread to make the meal more substantial.
A poached or fried egg makes an excellent addition.
The salad can be served warm, lukewarm or at room temperature.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well, although the fresh herbs should ideally be added after reheating.
notes.leftovers: Excellent cold or gently reheated. Leftovers can be used as a filling for wraps or served with a fried egg.
